user_query:
We're planning Q3 inventory for 22 stores and need a full demand planning package across our top ~120 SKUs. Please read all the following files under input/:
- input/sales_history.csv (weekly units by sku, location for the past 104 weeks; include columns: sku, location, week_start (YYYY-MM-DD), units, promo_flag, price)
- input/inventory.csv (current inventory position by sku-location; include columns: sku, location, on_hand, on_order, backorders, committed)
- input/vendors.csv (supply parameters; include columns: sku, vendor, lead_time_days_avg, lead_time_days_std, case_pack, moq_units, review_policy (continuous|min_max|periodic), review_period_days (if periodic), ordering_cost)
- input/service_levels.yaml (target service levels by segment and any overrides; keys may include AX/AY/AZ/BX/BY/BZ/CX/CY/CZ and/or per-sku overrides)
- input/costs.csv (cost parameters; include columns: sku, unit_cost, holding_cost_per_unit_year)
- input/sku_attributes.csv (classification inputs; include columns: sku, category, margin_pct, seasonality_flag)
- input/promotions.csv (upcoming events; include columns: sku, location, start_week (YYYY-MM-DD), end_week (YYYY-MM-DD), mechanic (TPR|display|circular|combo), discount_pct, cross_promoted_sku)

Deliverables – write all outputs under output/ with these exact filenames and schemas:
1) output/forecasts.csv: Forecast the next 8 selling weeks by sku and location. Columns: sku, location, week_start, baseline_fcst_units, promo_lift_units, total_fcst_units, lower_ci, upper_ci, method. Requirements: total_fcst_units must equal baseline_fcst_units + promo_lift_units per row; choose methods by demand pattern (e.g., Holt-Winters for seasonal, Holt for trend, Croston/SBA for intermittent), remove promo effects from baseline, and include a 90–95% CI range.
2) output/safety_stock.csv: Columns: sku, location, service_level, z, demand_sigma_weekly, lead_time_days, lead_time_sigma_days, review_period_days, safety_stock_units, formula_used. Use service_levels.yaml for targets; include lead time variability. If periodic review is used, include review period in the risk window.
3) output/replenishment.csv: Columns: sku, location, inventory_position_units, reorder_point_units, order_up_to_units, eoq_units, case_pack, recommended_order_units, policy. Compute inventory_position_units = on_hand + on_order − backorders − committed. Ensure recommended_order_units is 0 if inventory_position_units ≥ reorder_point_units; otherwise a non-negative multiple of case_pack, and respect MOQ if binding. Align policy with vendors.csv review_policy (ROP/EOQ for continuous, min/max, or periodic (R,S)).
4) output/classification.csv: Columns: sku, abc_class, xyz_class, margin_contribution_dollars, cv_demand. Do ABC on margin dollars (80/15/5 rule of thumb) and XYZ on de-seasonalized, de-promoted demand CV (<0.5 X, 0.5–1.0 Y, >1.0 Z).
5) output/recommendations.md: A concise planning memo (<= 2 pages) with these sections (use headings): Assumptions; Forecast Method Choices; Promo Plan (explicitly call out lift, cannibalization, and post-promo dip modeling); Markdown Recommendations (for any weak seasonal or overstocked items, with margin impact); Slow-Mover Actions (weeks-of-supply thresholds and exit timing); Risks & Escalations (lead time shocks, forecast bias triggers, service level risks). Reference specific SKUs where relevant.

Additional guidance:
- Use analogous-item variability only where history is insufficient. Default service levels by segment if not overridden: A=95%, B=95%, C=90% (tune per XYZ per best practice). Document any assumptions in the memo.
- Round EOQ and orders to vendor case_pack; if case_pack is missing or 0, assume 1. For periodic policies, compute S over (review_period_days + lead_time_days) plus safety stock.
- Ensure all outputs cover the same set of active sku-location pairs present in inventory.csv and reflect upcoming promotions from input/promotions.csv.

When done, confirm the file paths you wrote and any caveats, then provide a short executive summary inline.
